content.full_text:
George A. Johnson
Lampoc Realty
410 E. Ocean Ave.
Lampoc, Ca. 93436
(805) 735-3377
May 12, 1990

FPPC

Mar 16 8 55 AM '90
90-391

Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan
California Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O.Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Donovan:

Thank you for your letter of May 10, 1990.

As the first day of obtaining political papers is July 1, 1990, there is not
that much hurry. Even if you do not meet the 21 day's mentioned, I doubt there
would be much trouble until the latter part of June, 1990.

I also have no problem my letter being made public record, as I fully intend to
follow this up in Federal Court under the 5th and 14th Amendments to the
Constitution, unless there are already cases proving that such an advantage to
encumbants is possible.

Again thank you for your letter.

Sincerely,

George A. Johnson

George A. Johnson
Lompoc Realty
410 E. Ocean Ave.
Lompoc, CA 93436
(805) 735-3377
May [illegible] 9903 08 PM '90


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St.
Sacramento, Ca. 95804-0814

Dear sirs and madams:

I am hoping for a written response to the following inquiry before July 1,
1990, as that is the start of the filing time for the following offices. I
will try to give all the facts below. If you need more, please call me.

I live in the City of Lompoc, which I understand is a "general law city".
This November there will be 4 of the 5 seats up for election.
#1. The Mayor, elected at large for 2 years. This is a separate vote.
#2 and #3, are the regular council seats for 4 years. These are voted
together and the two highest vote getters are seated.
#4. This is a special election for a 2 year replacement of a council
member who had to resign because of leaving for another county.

I have called the City Clerk regarding running for all three races. She tells
me that she believes that I cannot do this because of the statement which I
must sign swearing that I will serve, if elected, Affidavit of Nominee and Oath
or Affirmation of Allegiance, General Law (20-30) 1988/0052e.

Can you please tell me your position on this matter. I am most interested in
any rulings from the Federal Courts, as being prohibited from running for more
than one election seems contrary to the 5th and 14th amendment to the
Constitution of the United States.

Sincerely,

[signature]
George A. Johnson
